pubmed_links: [Myung et al. (2003) Deranged hypothetical proteins Rik protein, Nit protein 2 and mitochondrial inner membrane protein, Mitofilin, in fetal Down syndrome brain](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/14528910/)
summary: NIT2 (omega-amidase) was found to be ~2-fold reduced at the protein level in fetal Down syndrome cortical brain tissue compared to controls in a proteomics study, representing the only direct link of NIT2 to a neurodevelopmental condition; no genetic association studies connect NIT2 to autism, schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, or intellectual disability. (novel candidate)
